evidence.title: Optimizing AI Spend with Agentic Workflow Transparency - LinkedIn
evidence.snippet: tying agentic workflows to CAC makes the cost conversation legible to non-technical leaders without oversimplifying it. Everyone is building agents, but almost nobody is thinking about how they actually get work done. An agent cannot complete a complex task without:. The agent with systems works 35 hours, closed 24 deals, and actually took a vacation without their business falling apart. In practice, a large number of business use cases can be modelled with single-agent, linear chaining workflows, rather than adaptive and complex patterns like reflection loops or multi-agent generator–optimizer setups. Here’s what decisions I’ve consistently seen work well when moving agentic projects from lab to production:. None of them said "I need AI." They said things like "I just want to know if I'm making money on this job" and "we only use a fraction of what we're paying for." And they all had an inkling that there may be a more AI-centric approach to improving their relationship to their business.

follow_up_query=Transparent ceo es automation agentic workflows

evidence.snippet: Hetikus is a legal technology startup company based in Spain. Since being founded, the company has gone through two rounds of startup funding, with a pair of investors being interested in the project. The company was founded in 2021 by Sergio Escosa and has its headquarters based in Barcelona, Catalonia, Spain, with an employee size of around one to ten workers. GuruCall is a Spanish startup that was founded in 2020 and is currently based in Madrid, Spain. DoGood People is a Spanish startup that was founded in 2019 and is currently based in the capital city of Spain. Collectively, these investors have helped the company to raise a total of 2.5 Million Euros in startup funding. Opground is a Spanish startup that was founded in 2019 and is currently based in Barcelona, Spain. MiResi is a Spanish startup that was founded in 2019 and is currently based in the city of Madrid, Spain.

evidence.title: CodeOp secures +€320k seed round to tackle gender disparity in the tech industry. - CodeOp
evidence.snippet:
Title: CodeOp secures +€320k seed round to tackle gender disparity in the tech industry. - CodeOp
# CodeOp secures +€320k seed round to tackle gender disparity in the tech industry. **Mission-led coding school, CodeOp, surpasses seed round goal to secure 320k+ to help change the face of the tech industry.**. CodeOp—the first and only coding school in Barcelona for women and the trans and gender non-conforming (TGNC) community—announces today that it has raised a seed round of over 320k, in-excess of its initial 260k goal, to offer coding bootcamps with a mission. Founded by Silicon Valley entrepreneur, Katrina Walker, CodeOp seeks to change the tech industry one bootcamp at a time. All-women coding schools make up less than 1% of all coding schools worldwide, a contributing factor to the current gender disparity in the tech industry. By offering beginner and advanced technical programs at its Barcelona campus, CodeOp aims to foster a supportive learning environment that encourages and equips people from these minority groups with the skills to become leading developers and data engineers. Though the school focuses on tech education for women and TGNC individuals, it works with individuals and communities worldwide, across and outside the gender spectrum, to foster a growing network of people who understand the issues these groups face. CodeOp receives the support of Lesbians Who Tech and SheCommerce, and has developed partnerships with global companies and organizations including Oracle and Fundació Catalana de l’Esplai. “There are a lot of statistics associated with women in tech.” Katrina Walker, CEO of CodeOp said. Investing in Codeop is a way to help level the playing field for women in tech. **For more information please contact yessica@staging.codeop.tech or +34 663 86 87 75**. CodeOp is the first international coding school for women and the TGNC community bridging the gap between technical education and development, and diversity and inclusion. With campuses in Barcelona and Kuala Lumpur, CodeOp offers beginner and advanced level bootcamps to students to equip them with the skills needed to become leading developers and data engineers in their fields. CodeOp, a Spanish-based social venture launched in 2018, is on a mission to encourage and support women, trans, and gender nonconforming individuals to pursue fulfilling careers in tech, and to launch a supportive network of professionals who will transform the culture.
